qa_pairs.question: What is the primary purpose of this document regarding software?
qa_pairs.answer: This document provides a current list of software available on our national systems and instructions on how to access or request it.

qa_pairs.question: How can users request new software installations or updates?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can request the installation or updating of a particular program or library by contacting technical support.

qa_pairs.question: If I want to use the software environment on my own system, where can I find instructions?
qa_pairs.answer: If you wish to use our software environment on your own system, please see the 'Accessing CVMFS' page.

qa_pairs.question: What is the status of `StdEnv/2020` on newer systems?
qa_pairs.answer: `StdEnv/2020` is now deprecated and hidden on the newer systems.

qa_pairs.question: How do users typically access most software on the national systems?
qa_pairs.answer: Users access most software by loading a module using the Lmod module system.

qa_pairs.question: Where are most Python modules provided, and how are they accessed?
qa_pairs.answer: Most Python modules are provided as binary wheels, stored under `/cvmfs/soft.computecanada.ca/custom/python/wheelhouse/`, rather than as Lmod modules.

qa_pairs.question: What is the recommended method for installing R or Perl packages?
qa_pairs.answer: It is recommended to install R or Perl packages in your personal or group file space.

qa_pairs.question: Is Docker available on the clusters, and if not, what is the alternative?
qa_pairs.answer: Docker is not available on our clusters, but Apptainer is available by loading the `apptainer` module, and Docker containers can be converted to Apptainer.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if I try to load a software module that requires a license I don't have?
qa_pairs.answer: Attempting to load the module for licensed software will give you instructions on what to do to obtain access.

qa_pairs.question: Why are some software packages only available at specific sites?
qa_pairs.answer: Some software packages are only available at one site or another due to licensing restrictions, and are installed locally rather than in CVMFS.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of standard software environments (StdEnv)?
qa_pairs.answer: Standard software environments (StdEnv) are used to group available packages, and in rare cases, a different StdEnv may be needed to access a particular version of a package.

qa_pairs.question: Are operating system-related packages like Autotools and Git installed as modules?
qa_pairs.answer: No, many packages related to the operating system, such as Autotools, Make, and Git, are part of the default environment and are not installed as modules.

qa_pairs.question: How can I find out the prerequisites for a globally-installed module?
qa_pairs.answer: For globally-installed modules, you can click on the `[Expand]` link in the `Description` column to see a list of prerequisites and brief notes.

qa_pairs.question: What are some of the software types used to categorize modules?
qa_pairs.answer: Software is tagged with types such as ai (artificial intelligence), bio (biology), chem (chemistry), geo (earth sciences), math (mathematics), phys (physics and engineering), and tools (languages and libraries).

qa_pairs.question: Which clusters is the Gaussian computational chemistry software available on as a site-specific package?
qa_pairs.answer: The Gaussian computational chemistry software is available on the Nibi and Fir clusters as a site-specific package.

qa_pairs.question: How can a research group on the Cedar cluster get a Galaxy instance?
qa_pairs.answer: Any group on Cedar can have one Galaxy instance, run under a shared account created by admins, by contacting technical support to set it up.

qa_pairs.question: Who is eligible to use SAS software on the Cedar cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: SAS on Cedar is licensed software belonging to users from the Alberta School of Business who are eligible to run it.
